story.iWant: phoneNumber ve password ile login olabilmek
story.soThat: access token ve refresh token alabileyim ve sisteme güvenli bir şekilde erişebileyim
story.tasks: - Task 1: LoginAdminDto ve AuthResponseDto oluştur (AC: 2, 8) - Task 2: TokenService oluştur ve token generation logic implement et (AC: 6, 7) - Task 3: AuthService.loginAdmin() method implement et (AC: 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9) - Task 4: AuthController.loginAdmin() endpoint oluştur (AC: 1, 8, 10) - Task 5: Rate limiting yapılandırması (AC: 10) - Task 6: JWT configuration doğrulama (AC: 6) - Task 7: Unit test yaz (AC: All) - Task 8: Integration test yaz (AC: 1, 2, 8, 9, 10)

story-context.acceptanceCriteria: AC-2.3.1: POST /auth/login/admin endpoint oluşturulmuş ve çalışıyor AC-2.3.2: LoginAdminDto validation yapılıyor (phoneNumber: E.164 format, password: required) AC-2.3.3: phoneNumber ile user bulunuyor ve role === 'admin' kontrolü yapılıyor (değilse 403 Forbidden) AC-2.3.4: phoneVerified === true kontrolü yapılıyor (false → 403 Forbidden: "Phone not verified") AC-2.3.5: password bcrypt.compare() ile validate ediliyor (mismatch → 401 Unauthorized: "Invalid credentials") AC-2.3.6: Valid credentials → Access token (JWT) generate ediliyor (15-60 min expiry, payload: sub, phoneNumber, domainID, roles) AC-2.3.7: Valid credentials → Refresh token (UUID) database'e kaydediliyor (7-30 day expiry) AC-2.3.8: Response döndürülüyor: { accessToken, refreshToken, user: UserResDto, expiresIn } AC-2.3.9: Invalid credentials → 401 Unauthorized (generic message: "Invalid credentials" - no phone/password disclosure) AC-2.3.10: Rate limiting uygulanıyor: 5 attempts / 15 min per IP (@nestjs/throttler)

docs.doc: Comprehensive technical specification for Story 2.3 including API endpoints (POST /auth/login/admin), data models (LoginAdminDto, AuthResponseDto), token architecture (JWT + UUID refresh tokens), admin login flow, security requirements (bcrypt, rate limiting, generic error messages), and acceptance criteria AC-2.3.1 through AC-2.3.10. Performance targets: <300ms p95 for admin login.

TokenService specification: JWT generation (15-60 min expiry, payload structure), Refresh token generation (UUID, database-stored, 7-30 day expiry), generateTokens() helper method. Dependencies: JwtService, PrismaService, ConfigService. Token rotation pattern for refresh flow.

Complete DTO specifications: LoginAdminDto (phoneNumber: E.164, password: required), AuthResponseDto (accessToken, refreshToken, user: UserResDto, expiresIn), JwtPayload interface (sub, phoneNumber, domainID, roles, iat, exp). Validation decorators: @IsPhoneNumber('TR'), @IsString, @IsNotEmpty.

doc.title: Epic 2 Technical Specification
doc.section: Workflows and Sequencing - Flow 2: Admin Login

docs.doc: Complete admin login workflow: Rate limit check (5/15min) → User lookup by phoneNumber → Role check (admin only) → Phone verification check → Password validation (bcrypt.compare) → Token generation → Response. Error handling: 401 for invalid credentials (generic), 403 for phone not verified or non-admin role, 429 for rate limit exceeded.

Password validation using bcrypt.compare() (~100-150ms), timing-safe comparison. Password policy: minimum 8 characters, at least 1 letter and 1 number. Generic error messages to prevent phone enumeration attacks ("Invalid credentials" for both invalid phone and wrong password).

@nestjs/throttler integration: 5 attempts / 15 minutes per IP address. In-memory storage for MVP (Redis-based distributed rate limiting in Phase 2). Prevents brute-force password attacks. Returns 429 Too Many Requests after limit exceeded.

Phone-based JWT authentication decision. Hybrid token architecture: JWT access tokens (stateless, 15-60 min) + database-stored refresh tokens (revokable, 7-30 days). Auth module structure: services (auth.service.ts, token.service.ts), strategies (jwt.strategy.ts), guards (jwt-auth.guard.ts), decorators (@Public()).

Complete implementation patterns for TokenService, AuthService.loginAdmin(), LoginAdminDto, AuthResponseDto, AuthController with code examples. Security considerations: generic error messages, rate limiting, phone verification enforcement, bcrypt password validation. Performance targets: phone lookup ~5ms, password comparison ~100-150ms, token generation ~50ms, total login time ~200-300ms.

AuthService ready with register() method, PrismaService and ConfigService injected. bcrypt already imported for password hashing. UserResDto available with @Exclude decorators. Database schema updated in migration 20251105122316 with phoneNumber, passwordHash, role, phoneVerified fields. Testing patterns established: 12 E2E tests passing.

artifact.reason: Existing service with register() method. Ready to add loginAdmin() method. Already imports bcrypt, PrismaService, ConfigService, OtpService. Pattern established: Use plainToInstance for UserResDto response sanitization.
Existing controller with POST /auth/register endpoint. Ready to add POST /auth/login/admin endpoint. Uses @Public() decorator pattern for authentication bypass.
JwtModule configured with async configuration. PassportModule registered. Need to add: TokenService to providers, ThrottlerModule import for rate limiting.
Response DTO that excludes passwordHash, deletedAt, domainID. Ready to be reused in AuthResponseDto for login response.
Decorator to bypass JWT authentication guard. Use for /auth/login/admin endpoint (authentication not required for login itself).
JWT payload structure: sub (user ID), phoneNumber, domainID, roles (array), iat, exp. Use as return type for JWT token generation in TokenService.
Extends AuthGuard('jwt'), checks @Public() metadata. Handles authentication errors. Already configured globally in AuthModule exports.
User model with phoneNumber (unique), passwordHash, role, phoneVerified, domainID fields. Use for user lookup and validation in loginAdmin().
RefreshToken model with userID, domainID, token (unique), expiresAt, createdAt. Use for storing refresh tokens in TokenService.generateRefreshToken().
Sets NODE_ENV=development, JWT_SECRET, JWT_ACCESS_EXPIRATION=15m, JWT_REFRESH_EXPIRATION=7d for E2E tests.
E2E test pattern established: beforeAll setup with AppModule, afterEach cleanup, 12 passing tests. Follow same pattern for auth-login-admin.e2e-spec.ts.
JWT environment variables already validated: JWT_SECRET (min 32 chars), JWT_ACCESS_EXPIRATION (default 15m), JWT_REFRESH_EXPIRATION (default 7d).

package.name: @nestjs/jwt
package.note: JWT token generation and signing. Already installed.

package.name: @nestjs/passport
package.note: Passport.js integration for NestJS. Already installed.

package.name: passport-jwt
package.note: JWT strategy for Passport. Already installed.

package.name: bcrypt
package.note: Password hashing and comparison. Already installed.

package.name: uuid
package.note: UUID generation for refresh tokens. Already installed.

package.name: @nestjs/throttler
package.note: Rate limiting middleware. MUST INSTALL for Story 2.3 AC-2.3.10.

story-context.constraints: 1. **Token Architecture:** JWT access tokens must be stateless (15-60 min expiry). Refresh tokens must be database-stored UUIDs (7-30 day expiry, revokable). 2. **Security - Generic Error Messages:** Always return "Invalid credentials" for authentication failures. Never disclose if phone exists or password is wrong (prevents phone enumeration). 3. **Security - Password Validation:** Use bcrypt.compare() for timing-safe comparison (~100-150ms). Never log or return plain-text passwords. 4. **Security - Phone Verification:** Enforce phoneVerified === true before allowing login. Return 403 Forbidden if not verified. 5. **Security - Role Enforcement:** Only allow role === 'admin' for this endpoint. Return 403 Forbidden for non-admin users. 6. **Rate Limiting:** Implement @nestjs/throttler with 5 attempts / 15 minutes per IP. Return 429 Too Many Requests after limit exceeded. 7. **Performance Target:** Admin login must complete in <300ms (p95). Phone lookup: ~5ms (indexed), password comparison: ~100-150ms (bcrypt), token generation: ~50ms. 8. **Module Organization:** Place TokenService in src/modules/auth/services/, DTOs in src/modules/auth/dto/. Follow NestJS best practices. 9. **Response Format:** Use { success: boolean, data: AuthResponseDto } format. Exclude passwordHash, deletedAt, domainID from user response. 10. **Testing Requirements:** Write unit tests for TokenService and AuthService.loginAdmin(). Write E2E tests covering all acceptance criteria including rate limiting. 11. **JWT Configuration:** Use ConfigService for JWT_SECRET, JWT_ACCESS_EXPIRATION, JWT_REFRESH_EXPIRATION. All values validated in env-validation.schema.ts. 12. **Database Operations:** Use PrismaService for User lookup (findUnique by phoneNumber) and RefreshToken creation. Leverage existing indexes on phoneNumber (unique) and token (unique).

interface.name: POST /auth/login/admin
interface.signature: @Public() @Throttle(5, 900) @Post('login/admin') async loginAdmin(@Body() loginDto: LoginAdminDto): Promise<{ success: boolean; data: AuthResponseDto }>

interface.name: TokenService.generateTokens
interface.signature: async generateTokens(user: User): Promise<{ accessToken: string; refreshToken: string; expiresIn: number }>

interface.name: LoginAdminDto
interface.signature: class LoginAdminDto { @IsPhoneNumber('TR') @IsNotEmpty() phoneNumber: string; @IsString() @IsNotEmpty() password: string; }

interface.name: AuthResponseDto
interface.signature: class AuthResponseDto { accessToken: string; refreshToken: string; user: UserResDto; expiresIn: number; }

interface.name: JwtPayload
interface.signature: interface JwtPayload { sub: string; phoneNumber: string; domainID: string; roles: string[]; iat: number; exp: number; }

interface.name: User Model
interface.signature: model User { id String @id @default(uuid()); phoneNumber String @unique; passwordHash String?; role String @default('staff'); phoneVerified Boolean @default(false); domainID String @db.Uuid; ... }

interface.name: RefreshToken Model
interface.signature: model RefreshToken { id String @id @default(uuid()); userID String @db.Uuid; domainID String @db.Uuid; token String @unique; expiresAt DateTime; createdAt DateTime @default(now()); ... }

tests.standards: Testing framework: Jest with @nestjs/testing. Unit tests: Mock PrismaService, JwtService, ConfigService. E2E tests: Use real database with test data cleanup in afterEach. Test structure: beforeAll (setup app module), afterEach (cleanup test data), afterAll (close app). Validation: Test all acceptance criteria, error scenarios, edge cases. E2E test pattern established in test/auth-registration.e2e-spec.ts (12 passing tests). Coverage target: All acceptance criteria covered.
tests.locations: Unit tests: src/modules/auth/services/token.service.spec.ts (NEW), src/modules/auth/auth.service.spec.ts (EXTEND) E2E tests: test/auth-login-admin.e2e-spec.ts (NEW)
tests.ideas:
**TokenService Unit Tests:** - generateAccessToken() returns valid JWT with correct payload (sub, phoneNumber, domainID, roles) - generateRefreshToken() stores UUID token in database with correct expiresAt - generateTokens() returns both access and refresh tokens with correct expiresIn
**AuthService.loginAdmin() Unit Tests:** - Valid admin credentials returns AuthResponseDto with tokens - Invalid password throws UnauthorizedException("Invalid credentials") - Non-admin role throws ForbiddenException("Access denied") - Unverified phone throws ForbiddenException("Phone not verified") - Non-existent phone throws UnauthorizedException("Invalid credentials") - Generic error messages (no phone/password disclosure)
**E2E Tests:** - POST /auth/login/admin with valid admin credentials → 200 OK, returns { accessToken, refreshToken, user, expiresIn } - accessToken is valid JWT (decode and verify payload structure) - refreshToken is stored in database with correct expiresAt - Invalid password → 401 Unauthorized with generic message - Non-admin user → 403 Forbidden - Unverified phone → 403 Forbidden - Non-existent phone → 401 Unauthorized with generic message - Rate limiting: 6th attempt within 15 min → 429 Too Many Requests - Error messages are generic (no credential disclosure)
